title: docs: Add missing --url to first podman --remote example
body:
It does not actually work without that argument, looks like the default URL changed or something:

`Error: unable to connect to Podman. failed to create sshClient: connection to bastion host (ssh://core@localhost:43325/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock) failed: dial tcp [::1]:43325: connect: connection refused`

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

As you see here
$ podman --remote info
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using podman system connection list, or try podman machine init and podman machine start to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v4.1.1/libpod/_ping": dial unix ///run/user/3267/podman/podman.sock: connect: connection refused

Podman already defaults to /run/user/UID/podman/podman.sock?

> Podman already defaults to /run/user/UID/podman/podman.sock?

I just figured out the problem: So on my supposed "completely fresh install of Fedora Workstation 36 " I had in parallel been playing with and exploring learning `podman machine` - because `podman-remote` told me to do so! 😉  But then initially that didn't quite work for me, as per #14809. But it looks like that made my `$HOME/.config/containers/containers.conf` automatigally (I didn't manually add it myself) contain this:

```
[engine]
  active_service = "podman-machine-default"
  [engine.service_destinations]
    [engine.service_destinations.podman-machine-default]
      uri = "ssh://core@localhost:43325/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock"
      identity = "/home/vorburger/.ssh/podman-machine-default"
```

And so then blindly following https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/remote_client.md didn't work for me.

So I slightly misinterpreted the root cause about the "default", but given that podman itself makes  a change which affects this "default", perhaps it would still be clearer in the tutorial with the `--url` parameter explicitly, to make sure that step just always works?
Ok thanks.  I think that error message might need to be cleaned up a bit when run on a Linux box, since Linux does not require podman machine like Windows and Macs do.
